title: ajuga ciliata var. glabrescens
intro: Ajuga ciliata var. glabrescens, also known as bugleweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and is native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as high blood pressure, fever, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and roots are used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to offer health benefits like reducing stress, improving sleep quality, and boosting the immune system. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, bugleweed is used to treat conditions like bronchitis, coughs, and sore throats, as well as to lower cholesterol levels and improve digestion.
